  "findings": [
    "Bearing inner ring exhibits bluing/discoloration indicating overheating during installation",
    "Thermal damage has compromised the metallurgical structure and hardness of the bearing steel"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Reject the installed bearing assembly",
    "Remove the overheated bearing and scrap it to prevent accidental reuse",
    "Procure a replacement bearing and reinstall using controlled heating methods (e.g., induction heater with temp probe set below 120C/250F)"
  ],
  "rationale": "The bearing inner ring shows clear signs of overheating (bluing) during the shrink-fit installation process. According to SKF and Timken mounting guides, heating a bearing above recommended limits (typically 120°C / 250°F) alters the metallurgy, reduces hardness, and will lead to premature, catastrophic bearing failure in service.",
  "references": [
    "SKF Bearing Installation and Maintenance Guide",
    "Timken Bearing Damage Analysis Guide (Overheating/Thermal Damage)"
  ]
}
